text.title: Fine-tune vs prompt decision tree — core rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every decision artefact must satisfy.

rule.statement: All four axes (quality, cost, latency, maintenance) are scored on a 1–5 scale with a one-line justification each. Missing axis → block the decision.
rule.rationale: One-axis decisions (just cost, just quality) systematically pick FT when a cheaper path exists.

rule.statement: Alternatives tried are enumerated with their measured eval lift each: at minimum {prompt-improve, RAG, routing, distillation}. Untried alternatives must be marked "untried" not omitted.
rule.rationale: Skipping enumeration lets the team forget that prompt-improve was never properly attempted.

rule.statement: Fine-tune recommendation requires ≥2 of 4 axes scored "below target" (score ≤2) AND ≥1 axis where no alternative cleared the bar. Otherwise the recommendation is the cheapest alternative that did clear the bar.
rule.rationale: Pre-commits the team to a real bar instead of vibes-based "FT will help".

rule.statement: Record declares ≥2 named revisit triggers (e.g. "volume crosses 100k req/day", "Sonnet 4.6 ships at -50% price", "eval drift > 5%"). Without triggers the decision becomes immortal.
rule.rationale: Pricing and capability shift every quarter; a 2-year-old FT decision is almost always wrong.

rule.statement: A named human owner signs the record. `owner` MUST NOT be "team", "everyone", or "TBD".
rule.rationale: Decisions without an owner orphan; nobody re-evaluates at trigger time.

rule.statement: All four axes scored, alternatives enumerated, ≥2 axes failing OR cheapest-passing alternative recommended, ≥2 revisit triggers, owner named.
No eval set OR compliance forces FT OR research-only — record cannot be authored meaningfully.
